claims_verified.claim: kickoff.sh captures a non-flag token after --new as BRIEF, keeps it out of REST, forwards it only to --phase new, and preserves spaces/glob chars intact
claims_verified.evidence: manual bash trace of the exact parsing loop (scripts/kickoff.sh:74-88) piped into a fake argv-printer: brief 'fix * and ? globs' and 'hello   world' arrived as single, unexpanded argv tokens; --skip-clarify stayed in REST only

claims_verified.claim: _default_author_spec appends the brief block only when brief is truthy, keeping the no-brief prompt byte-identical
_refresh_stub_spec's stale-stub detection still recognizes a failed --new left over when a brief was supplied
claims_verified.evidence:
REFUTED. orchestrator/run.py:240 hardcodes `stub = f"# {task_id}
"`, never updated to match the brief-inclusive seed introduced at run.py:214. Reproduced with a standalone test (added temporarily, confirmed failing, then reverted): a failed `--new --brief "add rate limiting"` leaves worktree spec `'# t9

add rate limiting
'`; after pushing the real spec to hub main and re-running `--phase clarify`, the worktree spec is still `'# t9

add rate limiting
'` instead of picking up 'the real ask' from hub — the exact self-heal `_refresh_stub_spec` exists to perform, silently broken whenever `--brief` was used on the failed attempt.

claims_verified.claim: full gate passes on this branch
claims_verified.evidence: not re-run in this pass; rw1 reported ruff/basedpyright/pytest all green, unaffected by this finding since no existing test exercises brief+stub-refresh together

findings.summary: _refresh_stub_spec's hardcoded bare-headline stub no longer matches the brief-inclusive seed, so the hub-refresh self-heal silently no-ops whenever a failed `--new` used `--brief`.
findings.failure_scenario:
kickoff t9 --new "add rate limiting" fails to author anything -> worktree stub is `# t9

add rate limiting
`. The real spec is later pushed to hub main. A later plain `kickoff t9` reuses the worktree; `_refresh_stub_spec` compares against the literal `f"# {task_id}
"`, doesn't match, returns early, and clarify proceeds against the stale brief-only stub instead of the real spec. Reproduced directly against this branch.

test_assessment: New tests (test_kickoff_wiring.py, test_run_cli.py) thoroughly cover the routing and seeding behavior this spec targets (AC1-4, AC6), but none exercise the interaction between a brief-inclusive failed-`--new` stub and `_refresh_stub_spec`'s existing recovery path (already covered for the no-brief case by test_clarify_refreshes_stub_spec_from_hub). That gap is exactly what let this regression through untested.
residual_risks: AC5 (flag-looking token after --new not swallowed as brief) still has no dedicated automated test, only manual verification.

Once _refresh_stub_spec is fixed to recognize the brief-inclusive stub, the existing bare-headline test and a new brief-variant test should both be run to confirm no regression in the surgical 'leave non-stub content alone' behavior (test_clarify_leaves_non_stub_spec_alone).
